generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Bidirectional actuator integrated into Building Control System (BCS) for lighting systems, enabling automated control via Eclipse Ditto middleware. Device configurations (tenant IDs, endpoint addresses) embedded as metadata within BIM models."
]

instance HVACUnitActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC system actuator integrated with BCS for temperature/ventilation control. Domain-specific control semantics (e.g., setpoints, scheduling) embedded in BIM metadata for automated deployment via Eclipse Ditto."
]

instance AdaptiveShadingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Smart shading mechanism actuator with bidirectional control capabilities. Device configurations (blind positions, tenant-specific profiles) stored as BIM metadata for runtime synchronization."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT temperature sensor deployed within building infrastructure capturing real-time operational data. Sensor configuration (ID, data format, sampling rate) embedded as BIM metadata and automatically extracted via Eclipse Hono during deployment."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT humidity sensor integrated with BCS for environmental monitoring. Post-occupancy data used for iterative refinement of control policies as demonstrated in BOREALIS project."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Presence detection sensor providing real-time occupancy data for space utilization analysis. Sensor metadata automatically extracted from BIM during deployment pipeline."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Smart meter sensor capturing energy consumption metrics for predictive maintenance and optimization. Data ingested via Eclipse Hono for real-time processing in digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnergyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BuildingInformationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "BIM model serving as static semantic backbone containing geometric, topological, and device configuration metadata. Enriched with runtime semantics for operational monitoring and control."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoConfigurator, BIMValidationTool
]

instance OperationalRuntimeData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational data including sensor readings, control signals, and system events ingested via Eclipse Hono. Used for monitoring, analysis, and control policy execution."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnergyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoRuntimeEngine, UnrealEngineVisualizer
]

instance ControlPolicyModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Domain-specific control policies (lighting schedules, HVAC setpoints) embedded as metadata within BIM models. Automatically deployed via Eclipse Ditto for runtime execution."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoConfigurator
]

instance EnergyPerformanceSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model predicting energy consumption trends and equipment failure risks. Used for predictive maintenance and optimization services."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data ingestion and device management platform enabling bidirectional communication between physical sensors and digital twin. Automatically extracts sensor metadata from BIM during deployment."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIngestionService
]

instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Service-oriented framework for device twin management, bidirectional control logic execution, and runtime configuration management. Orchestrates multiple internal twins (device, control, analytics)."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlExecutionService
]

instance UnrealEngineVisualizer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D immersive visualization platform enabling interactive monitoring of building operations through BIM-based digital twin representation."
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveVisualizationService
]

instance DittoConfigurator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven tool for automated extraction of device configurations from BIM models and generation of runtime artifacts for Eclipse Ditto deployment."
    DTDFVocab:enables AutomatedDeploymentService
]

instance BIMValidationTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Semantic validation tool ensuring consistency of BIM models before deployment into digital twin pipeline."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelValidationService
]

instance ReinforcementLearningEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AI engine for iterative refinement of control policies using post-occupancy sensor data as demonstrated in BOREALIS project."
    DTDFVocab:enables PolicyOptimizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous operational monitoring service providing real-time insights into building performance metrics through sensor data analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingPerformanceInsight, EquipmentHealthAlert
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics service identifying potential equipment failures and energy consumption trends using simulation models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailureRiskInsight, EnergyOptimizationRecommendation
]

instance AdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated control logic deployment service for lighting, HVAC, and adaptive systems with runtime configuration management capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlPolicyAdjustment, OccupancyBasedAdaptation
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "3D immersive visualization service enabling facility managers to monitor building operations through Unreal Engine interface."
    DTDFVocab:provides SpatialPerformanceInsight, TenantSpecificDashboard
]

instance PolicyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "AI-driven control policy optimization service using reinforcement learning to refine operational strategies based on post-occupancy data."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizedControlPolicy, EnergySavingsInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational performance metrics including energy consumption, occupancy patterns, and system efficiency indicators."
]

instance EquipmentHealthAlert : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive alerts indicating potential equipment failures based on sensor data analysis and simulation models."
]

instance ControlPolicyAdjustment : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of control policies (lighting schedules, HVAC setpoints) without full redeployment."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OccupancyBasedAdaptation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adaptation of building systems based on real-time occupancy data for optimized energy usage."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FailureRiskInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative risk assessment of equipment failures with recommended maintenance actions."
]

instance EnergyOptimizationRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven suggestions for energy consumption reduction based on predictive modeling."
]

instance OptimizedControlPolicy : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "AI-generated control policies that maximize energy efficiency while maintaining occupant comfort."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergySavingsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Projected energy savings estimates resulting from optimized control policies."
]

instance SpatialPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D spatial visualization of building performance metrics integrated with BIM geometry."
]

instance TenantSpecificDashboard : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Customizable dashboards providing tenant-specific metrics like comfort levels and energy usage."
]

// C12: Environment
instance BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Centralized yet modular digital twin environment containing all components for building infrastructure monitoring and control."
    base:contains BuildingInformationModel, OperationalRuntimeData, ControlPolicyModel, EnergyPerformanceSimulation,
                 TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor, OccupancySensor, EnergyConsumptionSensor,
                 LightingActuator, HVACUnitActuator, AdaptiveShadingActuator
]

// C13: Components
instance DeviceTwinOrchestrator : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Central component managing 1:N relationships between BIM model instances and multiple device twins for scalable infrastructure monitoring."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance ControlLogicExecutor : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Runtime component executing domain-specific control policies (lighting, HVAC) with bidirectional communication to physical actuators."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance AnalyticsProcessingUnit : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Modular component performing predictive maintenance and energy optimization analysis using post-occupancy sensor data."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance VisualizationRenderer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "3D visualization component rendering immersive building performance metrics through Unreal Engine interface."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

// Additional required instances for relationships
instance DittoRuntimeEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Runtime engine for executing control logic and managing device twins within Eclipse Ditto framework."
]

instance DataIngestionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service responsible for real-time data ingestion from physical sensors via Eclipse Hono."
]

instance ControlExecutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing control logic commands to physical actuators through Eclipse Ditto."
]

instance ModelValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating BIM models for semantic consistency before deployment."
]

instance AutomatedDeploymentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service automating the deployment of device twins and control policies from BIM models."
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service performing predictive analytics using simulation models and sensor data."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Virtual-to-physical interaction is achieved through **runtime control logic** embedded in the DT, which generates **actuation commands** for physical systems via the **Eclipse Ditto middleware**. The framework enables **bidirectional communication** between the DT and the BCS, allowing the DT to **adjust lighting, HVAC, or other adaptive systems** based on real-time analytics. The **TwinLight project** introduces a **layered system architecture** where BIM models are coupled with middleware for **automated control logic deployment**, ensuring that **control signals** (e.g., lighting adjustments) are **directly fed back** to physical actuators. The **BOREALIS project** further extends this by using **reinforcement learning** to optimize control policies, demonstrating the DT’s ability to **dynamically adjust physical systems** based on virtual simulations."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time and event-driven**, with **millisecond-to-second granularity** for critical control operations (e.g., lighting adjustments) and **minute-to-hour intervals** for broader analytics (e.g., energy consumption trends). The **Eclipse Hono and Ditto middleware** ensure **low-latency data exchange**, enabling **sub-second synchronization** between physical sensors and the DT. The **BOREALIS project** further emphasizes **iterative optimization cycles**, where control policies are refined **hourly to daily** based on post-occupancy data. The framework supports **asynchronous event processing** for non-critical updates (e.g., occupancy changes) while maintaining **strict real-time constraints** for control actions."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **service-oriented architecture** with **Eclipse Ditto as the central hub**, enabling **bidirectional communication** between physical systems and the DT. The constellation includes:
   - **BIM models** as the **semantic foundation**, enriched with **runtime metadata**.
   - **Eclipse Hono** for **sensor data ingestion** and **device authentication**.
   - **Unreal Engine 5** for **visualization and user interaction**.
   - **Custom services** (e.g., energy optimization, predictive maintenance) integrated via **modular extension points**.
   The framework supports **heterogeneous data sources** (e.g., IoT sensors, BCS protocols) and **cross-platform interoperability**, ensuring seamless integration with **existing AECO workflows**. The **modular design** allows **incremental deployment** of services, enabling **scalable, adaptive DT ecosystems**."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT operates across **all life cycle stages** of the building, from **design (as-designed)** to **construction (as-manufactured)** and **operation (as-operated)**. During **design**, the DT leverages **BIM models** for **clash detection and performance simulation**. In **construction**, it supports **automated device commissioning** via **Eclipse Hono**. During **operation**, the DT enables **real-time monitoring, control, and adaptive optimization** using **runtime sensor data**. The framework also accommodates **refurbishment and adaptation phases**, where **BIM models are updated** to reflect changes in the physical infrastructure, ensuring **continuous synchronization** between the DT and the PT. The **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric** is used to evaluate the DT’s fidelity across these stages."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
   - **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, which quantifies the **alignment between the DT and the physical twin (PT)** by evaluating **data consistency, synchronization latency, and control accuracy**.
   - **Automated validation** of BIM models to detect **semantic inconsistencies** (e.g., missing device configurations).
   - **Real-time error detection** via **Eclipse Ditto’s telemetry** for **anomaly monitoring** in sensor/actuator data.
   - **Post-occupancy data analysis** (as in **BOREALIS**) to **validate control policies** and **adjust models** iteratively.
   The framework accounts for **uncertainty in sensor data** and **model drift** (as discussed in Abbasi et al.) by incorporating **adaptive learning mechanisms** into the DT’s control logic."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT employs **Eclipse Ditto’s MQTT-based protocol** for **low-latency, bidirectional communication** between physical systems and the DT. Data is transmitted via **Eclipse Hono’s secure ingestion layer**, which handles **authentication, device management, and data normalization**. The framework supports **TCP/IP-based connections** for **real-time synchronization**, with **customizable QoS levels** (e.g., at-least-once delivery for critical control signals). The **Unreal Engine 5 integration** uses **RESTful APIs** for **visualization updates**, ensuring **seamless interaction** between the DT’s virtual and physical components."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is **hosted in a cloud-native environment**, leveraging **Eclipse Ditto’s Kubernetes-based deployment** for **scalability and fault tolerance**. The framework supports **on-premise or hybrid cloud hosting**, with **containerized microservices** (e.g., Ditto, Hono) ensuring **modular scalability**. The **Unreal Engine 5 runtime** is deployed as a **standalone client application** for **interactive visualization**, while **BIM models** are stored in **centralized repositories** (e.g., BIMserver) for **version control**. The framework adheres to **open-source licensing** (e.g., Eclipse Public License) to minimize **vendor lock-in** and enable **customizable deployment architectures**."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT facilitates **horizontal integration** with **external information systems** via:
   - **Standardized APIs** (e.g., REST, MQTT) for **interoperability** with **ERP, CMMS, or IoT platforms**.
   - **BIM integration** with **industry standards** (e.g., IFC, COBie) for **seamless data exchange** across AECO workflows.
   - **Modular service plugins** that enable **integration with third-party analytics** (e.g., AI-driven optimization tools).
   The framework supports **event-driven architectures** (e.g., via **Eclipse Ditto’s event bus**) to **sync with external systems** (e.g., **building management systems, energy grids**) without **direct coupling**. This ensures **flexible, scalable connectivity** with **heterogeneous IT landscapes**."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership is **shared between the building owner and DT service provider**, with **clear contractual agreements** defining **access rights and usage policies**. The framework adheres to **GDPR and AECO-specific privacy regulations**, ensuring:
   - **Encrypted data transmission** (e.g., TLS for MQTT) to protect **sensor and operational data**.
   - **Role-based access control (RBAC)** via **Eclipse Ditto**, restricting **data exposure** to authorized stakeholders (e.g., facility managers, engineers).
   - **Anonymization of tenant-specific data** where required to comply with **privacy laws**.
   The framework does not specify **explicit data ownership models** beyond **collaborative governance**, leaving **customization** to project-specific agreements."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **multiple industry standards** to ensure **interoperability and scalability**:
   - **Building Information Modeling (BIM)**: Uses **IFC (Industry Foundation Classes)** and **COBie (Construction Operations Building Information Exchange)** for **semantic consistency**.
   - **Eclipse Ditto**: Complies with **OPC UA** and **MQTT** for **device connectivity**.
   - **Eclipse Hono**: Follows **IoT security standards** (e.g., **X.509 certificates, JWT authentication**) for **secure data ingestion**.
   - **Unreal Engine 5**: Supports **standardized 3D visualization protocols** (e.g., **glTF, USDZ**) for **cross-platform compatibility**.
   The framework also integrates **modular extension points** to **comply with future AECO standards** (e.g., **BIM 4D/5D for time/cost integration**)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
   - **Eclipse Ditto’s built-in security** (e.g., **TLS 1.3, OAuth 2.0, JWT tokens**) for **authenticated communication**.
   - **Eclipse Hono’s device authentication** (e.g., **X.509 certificates**) to prevent **unauthorized access** to physical systems.
   - **Role-based access control (RBAC)** to restrict **DT manipulation** to authorized personnel (e.g., **facility managers, engineers**).
   - **Fail-safe mechanisms** in control logic to **prevent accidental actuation** (e.g., **emergency stop overrides**).
   The framework does **not explicitly address remote emergency operations**, but **control policies are designed to prioritize safety** (e.g., **fallback to manual modes** in case of DT failures). **Cybersecurity risks** (e.g., **DT hijacking**) are mitigated via **network segmentation** and **audit logging** in Ditto/Hono."
]
